title: Offline vs online evaluation
description: Offline eval on a fixed dataset is reproducible but may not reflect production; online eval measures real impact but is noisy and risky.
body: Use when deciding how to measure a change. Offline evaluation scores a system on a frozen dataset: it is fast, reproducible, and safe but only as representative as the data. Online evaluation (A/B, canary) measures real user impact but is noisy, slow, and exposes users to a possibly worse system. Do not ship on offline gains alone for a high-impact change, and avoid online tests with no offline gate first. Actions: gate with offline eval, then confirm with a controlled online test. Validation signals include an offline regression suite plus an online metric with a guardrail. Risk boundary: a large change validated only offline can regress production; require an online confirmation with a rollback for high-impact changes.
